summary.value: Daiichi Sankyo's FY2025 (Q4) results and 5-year business plan presentation on 2026-05-11 disclosed that patritumab deruxtecan 'did not demonstrate positive results' in the EGFR-mutated lung cancer setting — a program Daiichi had previously positioned as a third pillar alongside Enhertu and Datroway. Management revised the drug's launch timing without disclosing a new date, and the company's profit-forecast cut was linked in part to the US withdrawal and an ADC supply-strategy shift. The earnings-call readout of the same event adds that competing Daiichi ADC programs (I-DXd, R-DXd) are being accelerated by contrast; that launch-timing detail reached us through an aggregator paraphrase (AllSci, 2026-05-13) of the call and is reported here as such. This follows the May 2025 voluntary BLA withdrawal after HERTHENA-Lung02 missed overall survival — an event outside this coverage window and cited here only as context.
